---
title: "Computing Concepts with Python"
subtitle: "Open-access course materials by Brendan Shea, PhD"
toc: false
---

A free, open-access introduction to computer science, built around Python
notebooks, short case studies, and small interactive tools. Used in
**COMP 1150** at Rochester Community and Technical College and freely
adaptable elsewhere under [CC BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/).

## Start here

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-md-6 .g-col-12}
### For students

Read the [course landing page](summer_2026/index.qmd) for the current
semester. It links the notebooks (one per week) and the case studies
(one per notebook). Each notebook is a Jupyter notebook you can open in
Google Colab; each case study is a short read with a runnable algorithm
and a debate to think about.
:::

::: {.g-col-md-6 .g-col-12}
### For instructors

Everything here is licensed CC BY 4.0 — adapt and remix freely. The
[course outline](summer_2026/index.qmd) shows the twelve-week sequence
and the design rationale. Source for every notebook and case study is on
[GitHub](https://github.com/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python);
case studies are written in Quarto so you can edit, re-render, and
re-skin them for your own course.
:::

:::

## What's here

- **[Summer 2026 course](summer_2026/index.qmd)** — twelve notebooks
  and twelve case studies covering algorithms, data representation,
  databases, networks, security, and AI ethics. The current version.
- **[Case studies](summer_2026/cases/index.qmd)** — short, discussion-
  ready pieces on the history, ethics, and unresolved arguments behind
  the technical material.
- **[Interactive tools](https://github.com/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/tree/main/tools)**
  — PyQuiz (Python practice via widgets) and ObjectQuest (a small
  RPG-style quiz game). Source on GitHub.
- **[Archive](archive/index.qmd)** — the earlier 2024 version of the
  course (twelve PDF case studies plus the original notebooks),
  preserved for anyone with existing links.

## License & contact

Content is licensed under
[Creative Commons Attribution 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/) —
share and adapt freely with credit.

Brendan Shea, PhD · brendan.shea@rctc.edu ·
[github.com/brendanpshea](https://github.com/brendanpshea)
